steps.expected: CpuBackend, AstBackend, TrigramIndex, GpuSidecar all documented and in code
steps.observed: All 4 backends present in both routing_policy.md and main.rs (CpuBackend:6, AstBackend:6, TrigramIndex:8, GpuSidecar:1 occurrences)
Document describes full priority: --index > --gpu-device-ids > warm-index > --json CPU > cold rg passthrough

assertions.title: Default text search routes to ripgrep

steps.action: Run tg search --verbose "hello" <dir> without index/gpu/json flags
Compare tg output with rg output
steps.observed: Both tg and rg returned identical lines (2 matches in test_ast.py)

assertions.title: Warm index auto-routing activates on compatible queries

steps.action: Run tg search --verbose "hello" <dir> (no --index flag, pattern >= 3 chars)
steps.observed: [routing] warm index found (2 files), using index-accelerated path; [routing] routing_backend=TrigramIndex routing_reason=index-accelerated sidecar_used=false

steps.action: Run tg search -v --verbose "hello" <dir> with warm index present
Run tg search -C 3 --verbose "hello" <dir> with warm index present

assertions.title: GPU sidecar routes on --gpu-device-ids

steps.action: Verify GPU routing takes priority over warm index
steps.observed: After rebuilding binary with routing fix (f8ef2f2), GPU correctly takes priority over warm index

evidence.consoleErrors: GPU backend unavailable: 'CuDF and Torch GPU backends were unavailable' (expected — routing is correct, GPU hardware/software not set up)

assertions.issues: GPU backend itself is unavailable (no CuDF/Torch), but routing correctly selects GpuSidecar. Per assertion instructions, this is PASS because routing is correct.

frictions.description: tg.exe binary was stale (built before commit f8ef2f2 'fix(routing): prioritize explicit gpu routing'). VAL-ROUTE-007 initially failed with warm index intercepting --gpu-device-ids. Had to rebuild binary with 'cargo build --release' before GPU routing test could pass.
frictions.resolution: Rebuilt tg.exe via 'cargo build --release' in rust_core/. After rebuild, --gpu-device-ids correctly takes priority over warm index.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: all 8 passed. VAL-ROUTE-001 confirmed routing_policy.md exists with all 4 backends cross-referenced against main.rs. VAL-ROUTE-002 through 006 confirmed correct CpuBackend/TrigramIndex routing decisions including warm index bypasses. VAL-ROUTE-007 confirmed GpuSidecar routing (GPU backend unavailable but routing correct). VAL-ROUTE-008 confirmed AstBackend for tg run. One friction: binary was stale and needed rebuild for VAL-ROUTE-007 to pass.
